refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy consumption per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and calculate energy_per_ton to verify it meets ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to teleoperation and safety systems to support energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy-efficient powertrain and control algorithms in Vehicle.
DP2: Implement real-time energy monitoring and reporting subsystem.
DP3: Develop teleoperation energy-aware task scheduling and control.
DP4: Safety Services to enforce operational policies that indirectly affect energy efficiency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy-efficient powertrain and control algorithms in Vehicle.

potential_sources: Energy monitoring data coupling with Teleoperation and Safety Services for feedback.
Control algorithm coupling with Vehicle hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates energy efficiency control within Vehicle, minimizing cross-module coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes Vehicle module can implement advanced control algorithms without excessive complexity.
Risk that energy monitoring accuracy depends on integration with Telemetry interface.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider enhancing DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address monitoring and teleoperation feedback explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for powertrain and energy management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reports energy consumption and operational data to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands from Teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy efficiency DP within Vehicle; low diffusion as control algorithms are centralized.
Limited containment; mainly receives data and sends commands, some diffusion risk if energy logic spreads here.
Minimal containment; indirect influence on energy efficiency through policy enforcement.
System-level coordination but no direct implementation of energy efficiency DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency control in Vehicle minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership and verification.
containment_risks: Potential diffusion if energy monitoring logic is duplicated in Teleoperation or Safety Services.
Interface dependency on Telemetry may introduce coupling risks.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define strict interface contracts and ownership boundaries to prevent diffusion; consider monitoring subsystem as separate IM in future refinement.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (energy-efficient powertrain and control algorithms) directly addresses the refined FR with clear measurable impact.

diffusion_risks: Risk of energy efficiency logic spreading into Teleoperation or Safety Services modules if not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: High containment score for Vehicle module supports strong ownership and manageable diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and operational testing is feasible within Vehicle and system-level integration.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency DP to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts for Telemetry and Tasking to avoid diffusion.
Plan next-level decomposition for energy monitoring and teleoperation feedback.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly address energy monitoring and teleoperation feedback as separate FRs and DPs to reduce diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to teleoperation and safety systems to support energy efficiency.

revise.why: To explicitly separate monitoring and teleoperation control aspects for clearer DP and IM assignments and reduce diffusion risks.

assumptions: Vehicle module can implement advanced control algorithms.
Telemetry interface provides accurate and timely data.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of energy efficiency logic across modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data from Vehicle control algorithms.
Telemetry data accuracy and latency metrics.
